questions.question:
Percy's conversation with his mother about his father serves as a turning point in the excerpt. How does this turning point propel the plot forward and hint at challenges Percy will face? 


Respond to this question in the form of a paragraph that includes a claim, evidence, and explanation.

responses.content: A challenge he will face is Being sent away again like it said in the passage that he did not want to move to a different school again after moving schools after so many times he has moved school so that will be a challenge.
responses.bucket_rationale: The response identifies a challenge Percy will face (being sent away again) and references the passage, but it only addresses the challenge part of the question and does not explain how the conversation propels the plot forward. The answer provides some evidence from the text but lacks a full explanation connecting the evidence to the claim. This fits Score 2: foundation is present, but only one part of the multi-part question is answered.

responses.content: A challenge he will face is Being sent away again like it said in the passage that he did not want to move to a different school again after moving schools after so many times he has moved school so that will be a challenge. What will happen next is that he will not try to escape from the places his mom what’s to send him to.
responses.bucket_rationale: The response addresses the challenge Percy will face (being sent away again) and uses evidence from the text about moving schools. The explanation labels the challenge but does not connect it to the plot movement, and the second part of the question (how the turning point moves the plot forward) is only briefly and vaguely addressed. Therefore, this fits Score 2, Pattern C: Secondary Part Skip.
